{"status":"ok","message-type":"work","message-version":"1.0.0","message":{"indexed":{"date-parts":[[2024,5,13]],"date-time":"2024-05-13T15:46:55Z","timestamp":1715615215998},"reference-count":0,"publisher":"Edinburgh University Press","isbn-type":[{"value":"9781474478816","type":"print"},{"value":"9781474496254","type":"electronic"}],"content-domain":{"domain":[],"crossmark-restriction":false},"short-container-title":[],"published-print":{"date-parts":[[2021,1,26]]},"abstract":"

This chapter discusses the assumptions that contemporary social media photographs are primarily expressive and the gendered implications of the language used to describe this kind of photography. Photographs are increasingly recognised as conversational, and the chapter contrasts this with modernist ideas about picture languages and photography as a rational language. It contextualises the changing uses and perceptions of images in relation to the growth of service culture in the twentieth century and in comparison to emoji. Images have become the central focus of critique of emotional media, and this chapter draws attention to the continuing role of gendered assumptions in discussions about images. These discussions deploy the recurrent metaphor of the flood to articulate a fear of being \"drowned\u201d out; they describe a decline of attention into a superficial \u201dgrazing\u201d; they repeat concerns about meaningless and incoherent speech or chatter, and exhibit an iconoclastic anxiety, in which images are understood as both deeply attractive and deeply dangerous.<\/p>","DOI":"10.3366\/edinburgh\/9781474478816.003.0005","type":"book-chapter","created":{"date-parts":[[2022,5,21]],"date-time":"2022-05-21T07:11:18Z","timestamp":1653117078000},"page":"77-96","source":"Crossref","is-referenced-by-count":0,"title":["5 Feeling Photos: Photography, Picture Language and Mood Capture"],"prefix":"10.3366","author":[{"given":"Henning","family":"Michelle","sequence":"first","affiliation":[],"role":[{"role":"author","vocabulary":"crossref"}]}],"member":"1931","published-online":{"date-parts":[[2022,5,19]]},"container-title":["Photography Off the Scale"],"original-title":["5 Feeling Photos: Photography, Picture Language and Mood Capture"],"language":"en","deposited":{"date-parts":[[2022,7,28]],"date-time":"2022-07-28T16:53:39Z","timestamp":1659027219000},"score":1,"resource":{"primary":{"URL":"https:\/\/academic.oup.com\/edinburgh-scholarship-online\/book\/42226\/chapter\/356345490"}},"subtitle":[],"short-title":[],"issued":{"date-parts":[[2021,1,26]]},"ISBN":["9781474478816","9781474496254"],"references-count":0,"URL":"https:\/\/doi.org\/10.3366\/edinburgh\/9781474478816.003.0005","relation":{},"subject":[],"published":{"date-parts":[[2021,1,26]]}}}
